file_insights.purpose: Integration test example script for Amazon SP-API MCF (Multi-Channel Fulfillment) operations

patterns_discovered.description: Uses a self-contained MCFIntegrationTest class that doesn't rely on pytest framework, making it runnable as a standalone script
Order creation test defaults to dry_run=True to prevent accidental inventory consumption
Tests are conditionally executed based on availability of SKU data and previous test results
Each test method catches MCFClientError and returns boolean success/failure status
locations: All test methods with try-except blocks

patterns_discovered.description: Uses checkmarks (✓), cross marks (✗), and warnings (⚠) for visual test result indication
Argparse configuration with comprehensive examples showing different usage scenarios

gotchas_discovered.description: The script creates a realistic order object but by default doesn't submit it. Developers must explicitly set create_real_order=True to actually consume inventory, with clear warnings about charges
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Default dry_run=True and explicit warnings in documentation

gotchas_discovered.description: Script requires running from ai-content-agents directory and manually adds parent directory to sys.path. ImportError provides helpful context but could fail if directory structure changes
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Clear error message and documentation about required execution directory

gotchas_discovered.description: Several tests are conditionally skipped if test_sku is not provided, which could give false sense of passing tests when key functionality isn't actually tested
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Documentation and warnings about what tests were skipped

gotchas_discovered.description: Script requires 6 environment variables to be configured (AMAZON_SELLER_ID, SP_API credentials, etc.) but doesn't validate all are present at startup
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: test_credentials() method checks required keys but only in first test

gotchas_discovered.description: List operations mention pagination with next_token but don't show how to actually use next_token for fetching additional pages
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Documentation notes pagination capability but doesn't implement full pagination loop

approach_outcome.delivery: Created comprehensive 473-line integration test script demonstrating real-world MCF operations
key_achievements: Implemented 6 integration tests covering full MCF workflow (credentials, inventory, orders, tracking)
Included dry-run safety mechanisms to prevent accidental charges during testing
Provided both programmatic API and CLI interface for flexibility
Added extensive documentation and user-friendly output formatting
Included realistic example data (addresses, order items) for reference
Designed to work standalone without test framework dependencies

recommendations.recommendation: Add validation of all required environment variables at script initialization, not just during first test
recommendations.rationale: Early detection of configuration issues prevents test suite from running partially

recommendations.recommendation: Add inline examples for using pagination with next_token when listing orders
recommendations.rationale: Developers learning from this script should see complete pagination patterns

recommendations.recommendation: Add retry logic with exponential backoff for API calls that might be rate-limited
recommendations.rationale: Integration tests against external APIs should handle transient failures gracefully

recommendations.recommendation: Add explicit confirmation prompt when create_real_order=True is specified
recommendations.rationale: Prevents accidental inventory consumption and charges from command-line slip-ups

recommendations.recommendation: Document how to mock MCFClient for unit testing this integration test script itself
recommendations.rationale: CI/CD pipelines may want to test this script without hitting actual Amazon APIs

recommendations.recommendation: Consider adding batch operations examples (e.g., creating multiple orders, querying multiple SKUs in one call)
recommendations.rationale: Would demonstrate more realistic production usage patterns
